title: camelina microcarpa subsp. sylvestris
intro: Camelina microcarpa subsp. sylvestris, also known as wild gold-of-pleasure, is a plant species within the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its unique golden yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as isothiocyanates, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of camelina microcarpa subsp. sylvestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
